title: Singly linked representation

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Singly linked representation; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Singly linked representation
sources.claim: A singly linked list stores nodes with values and next links, supporting head updates without shifting contiguous elements.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A singly linked list stores nodes with values and next links, supporting head updates without shifting contiguous elements.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Traversal advances current=current.next until null while retaining the head reference. Your goal is to draw, traverse and implement a singly linked list representation in the selected language. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Array representation and operations; Selected-language readiness. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: A singly linked list stores nodes with values and next links, supporting head updates without shifting contiguous elements. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, nodes need explicit allocation, links, and cleanup; in C++, prefer RAII ownership such as unique_ptr where it fits; in Java, node references are garbage-collected but links and equality remain explicit; in Python, object references simplify storage but do not change linear traversal complexity.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Random access remains linear, ownership and deallocation differ by language, and a lost link can orphan the suffix. The draft therefore rejects: Linked lists provide constant-time access to any index because nodes have pointers.

includes: Model head, node value, next link and empty or singleton states.
Draw, traverse and implement a singly linked list representation in the selected language.

In C, nodes need explicit allocation, links, and cleanup; in C++, prefer RAII ownership such as unique_ptr where it fits; in Java, node references are garbage-collected but links and equality remain explicit; in Python, object references simplify storage but do not change linear traversal complexity.
excludes: Random access remains linear, ownership and deallocation differ by language, and a lost link can orphan the suffix.

outcomes: Draw, traverse and implement a singly linked list representation in the selected language.

examples.title: Singly linked representation: language-neutral trace

walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: Traversal advances current=current.next until null while retaining the head reference.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Singly linked lists provide sequential traversal; reaching index k requires following k links.

examples.title: Singly linked representation: implementation transfer
examples.situation: In C, nodes need explicit allocation, links, and cleanup; in C++, prefer RAII ownership such as unique_ptr where it fits; in Java, node references are garbage-collected but links and equality remain explicit; in Python, object references simplify storage but do not change linear traversal complexity.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, nodes need explicit allocation, links, and cleanup; in C++, prefer RAII ownership such as unique_ptr where it fits; in Java, node references are garbage-collected but links and equality remain explicit; in Python, object references simplify storage but do not change linear traversal complexity.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Random access remains linear, ownership and deallocation differ by language, and a lost link can orphan the suffix.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Linked lists provide constant-time access to any index because nodes have pointers.
